relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Zophar answers again.
His thoughts rush because he feels provoked.
He answers from a spirit of insulted understanding.
He appeals to what he thinks has always been known.
The joy of the wicked, he says, is short.
Even if they rise to clouds,
they vanish in shame.
They disappear like a dream of the night.
Their place no longer sees them.
Their children must seek favor from the poor.
Youth itself descends with them to dust.
Evil may taste sweet in the mouth.
It may be held under the tongue.
But Zophar says it becomes venom within.
Swallowed wealth is forced back out.
Poison imagery fills his warning.
The wicked lose the streams of abundance.
Labor and wealth do not become joy.
Because the poor were crushed and homes seized.
Restless appetite cannot escape itself.
Greed leaves no enduring prosperity.
Abundance turns into distress.
Anger becomes the food of judgment.
Weapons overtake the fleeing one.
Terrors come upon him.
Darkness and unblown fire consume what remains.
Heaven and earth reveal guilt.
The increase of the house flows away.
Zophar calls this the appointed portion of the wicked.
